required_boundary: Escalate before including any other user's identifier in an export
Keep the export scoped to the requesting user's own data until consent/authorization is recorded

protected_concerns.source_fact: The export currently includes only the requesting user's own account data; no consent or data-sharing agreement authorizes exposing another user's personal identifiers inside someone else's export.
protected_concerns.invariant: A user's export must not disclose another user's personal identifiers without that person's own authorized consent.

required_boundary: Escalate the default-visibility decision before showing activity timestamps to all members
Keep the directory showing only name and role until the visibility default is approved

protected_concerns.source_fact: The team directory currently shows name and role only; activity/presence data is not exposed to other members and no visibility-default policy exists.
protected_concerns.invariant: Exposing a member's activity data to the whole workspace requires an explicit visibility-default decision, not a silent default-on rollout.

required_boundary: Escalate before granting the support role write access to billing data
Keep the support role read-only on billing until an approval workflow and audit trail are authorized

protected_concerns.source_fact: The support role currently has read-only billing access; no audit trail, approval workflow, or reversal path exists for a support-initiated billing edit.
protected_concerns.invariant: Granting a role write access to billing data requires an authorized approval workflow and audit trail before rollout, not an inline permission bump.

required_boundary: Escalate before letting a token read any project beyond the one it was created in
Keep existing tokens scoped to their original project until a per-project grant mechanism is authorized

protected_concerns.source_fact: API tokens are strictly scoped to the project they were created in; no cross-project trust relationship or consent step exists between sibling projects.
protected_concerns.invariant: A token's authorization boundary must not silently expand to other projects without an explicit per-project grant.

required_boundary: Escalate before changing how tax is rounded on posted invoices
Keep existing invoices and historical reports on the current per-line rounding method until migration/reversal approval is recorded

protected_concerns.source_fact: Posted invoices compute and round tax per line item; historical invoices and reports reproduce that method; no migration or restatement contract exists for already-issued invoices.
protected_concerns.invariant: Changing a posted financial calculation method must not alter the recorded meaning of already-issued invoices without migration and reversal authority.

required_boundary: Escalate before deleting any historical metering record
Keep all existing metering records and already-generated invoices unchanged until a reconciliation/adjustment process is authorized

protected_concerns.source_fact: Historical metering records are immutable once recorded and past invoices were already generated from them; no reconciliation or customer-adjustment process exists for retroactively deleting them.
protected_concerns.invariant: Retroactively deleting historical billing-relevant records must not happen without a reconciliation and customer-adjustment process.

required_boundary: Escalate before shortening the documented 30-day recovery window
Keep the existing 30-day recovery window in effect until the documentation and compliance dependencies are reconciled

protected_concerns.source_fact: Deleted workspaces currently remain recoverable for 30 days and existing customer-facing documentation states that window; backups and compliance exports assume the current window.
protected_concerns.invariant: Shortening an existing, documented recovery guarantee must not happen without reconciling existing customer commitments and compliance dependencies.

required_boundary: Escalate before repointing failover at a region that has not completed compliance review
Keep the current, compliance-reviewed region as the failover target until the new region's review completes

protected_concerns.source_fact: The current failover target region has passed compliance review; the proposed new region has not yet completed that review.
protected_concerns.invariant: The failover target must remain a compliance-reviewed region; changing it requires that review to complete first.

required_boundary: Escalate before backfilling the primary-contact field from an inferred heuristic
Leave the primary-contact field unset on existing accounts until a backfill/correction plan is authorized

protected_concerns.source_fact: Existing account records have no primary-contact field today; the proposed backfill source (most recent login) is a heuristic, not an authoritative source of the actual primary contact.
protected_concerns.invariant: Backfilling a new required field on existing records from an unauthoritative heuristic must not silently proceed without an approved backfill/correction plan.

required_boundary: Escalate before rebuilding the live index in place
Keep the existing index serving queries unchanged until a cutover plan is authorized

protected_concerns.source_fact: The existing search index continues to serve live queries; no dual-write, shadow-index, or documented cutover procedure between old and new schema exists today.
protected_concerns.invariant: Rebuilding a live-serving index to a new schema requires an authorized cutover plan so queries are never silently served from a partially migrated index.

required_boundary: Escalate before making downgrades effective immediately
Keep downgrades effective at the end of the billing period, as currently stated in billing terms, until a terms update and proration policy are authorized

protected_concerns.source_fact: Plan downgrades currently take effect at the end of the billing period; this timing is stated in existing customer-facing billing terms; no compensation or proration policy exists for an immediate-effect change.
protected_concerns.invariant: Changing an existing, customer-facing billing-timing commitment must not happen without an approved terms update and proration/compensation policy.

required_boundary: Escalate before treating admin silence as DPA renewal acceptance
Keep DPA renewal requiring an explicit admin re-signature until legal/product authorization for silence-as-acceptance is recorded

protected_concerns.source_fact: DPA renewal currently requires an explicit admin re-signature; no legal authorization exists for treating silence as acceptance of a contractual data-processing term.
protected_concerns.invariant: A binding contractual term must not switch from explicit consent to silence-as-acceptance without legal/product authorization.

required_boundary: Display a relative timestamp derived from the existing stored value
Do not change the underlying stored timestamp
Show a character counter reflecting the existing, already-enforced maximum length
Do not change the existing maximum length or validation behavior
Default the toggle to the existing Team option instead of leaving it unset
Keep the toggle changeable by the creator before and after saving, as today
unsupported_concepts: Bulk visibility change for existing projects

required_boundary: Reorder the three existing tabs so Notifications appears before Security
Keep all three tabs' contents and URLs unchanged
Add a copy-to-clipboard action next to the already-displayed key
Do not change who can view the key or how it is currently displayed
Show a loading skeleton exactly while the existing data request is pending
Do not change the underlying data request or its result
Increase row height on mobile viewports only
Keep the table's data, columns, and existing desktop layout unchanged
Show a warning at 90% of the existing, already-enforced character limit
Do not change the existing maximum length or server-side enforcement
Change only the visible button label to "Save changes"
Keep the form's existing submit behavior and validation unchanged
Add a collapse/expand control to the existing FAQ section
Keep the existing question and answer content and order unchanged
Use the existing, already-available name-sort option as the automatic tiebreaker for equal primary-sort values
Do not change the existing manual sort options or primary sort behavior
Add a column showing the existing, already-computed last-updated timestamp
Do not change how the timestamp is computed or who can already see it in other views
unsupported_concepts: Column reordering beyond adding the one column
